title: The Struggling Chai Chain
prompt: Your client is ChaiPoint, a fast-growing chai and snacks chain with 150 outlets across Tier 1 Indian cities. Over the past 18 months, their net margins have declined from 14% to 6% despite a 20% increase in total revenue. The CEO is alarmed and has hired your firm to diagnose the problem and recommend a path forward.
context: ChaiPoint operates both company-owned and franchised outlets. They recently expanded aggressively into mall and airport locations. They also launched a delivery channel via Swiggy and Zomato 14 months ago.

packet_1.label: Revenue by Channel
packet_1.content: Dine-in: 55% of revenue (was 80% 18 months ago). Delivery: 30% of revenue. Catering/B2B: 15% of revenue.
packet_1.release_trigger: candidate asks about revenue breakdown or channels

packet_2.label: Delivery Economics
packet_2.content: Average delivery order value: ₹220. Aggregator commission: 28%. Net revenue per delivery order: ₹158. Food cost per delivery order: ₹90. Contribution margin per delivery order: ₹68 before fixed allocation.
packet_2.release_trigger: candidate asks about delivery profitability or aggregator fees

packet_3.label: Location Cost Data
packet_3.content: Original high-street outlets: avg monthly rent ₹80,000. New mall outlets: avg monthly rent ₹3,20,000. Airport outlets: avg monthly rent ₹6,50,000. Mall and airport outlets represent 35% of total outlet count but 58% of total rental costs.
packet_3.release_trigger: candidate asks about fixed costs, rent, or location economics

packet_4.label: Competitor Benchmarks
packet_4.content: Comparable QSR chains in India operate at 10–16% net margins. Chains that limit delivery to under 20% of revenue report 2–3pp higher margins than delivery-heavy peers.
packet_4.release_trigger: candidate asks about industry benchmarks or competitors

hidden_answer_brief: The margin compression is driven by two compounding factors: (1) aggressive expansion into high-rent mall and airport locations that dramatically increased the fixed cost base, and (2) rapid growth of the delivery channel which carries structurally lower margins due to 28% aggregator commissions. Recommendation: rationalize the mall/airport expansion by closing or renegotiating leases on underperforming high-rent outlets, and cap delivery at 20% of revenue while investing in a direct ordering app to reduce aggregator dependency.
key_insights: Revenue growth can mask margin deterioration — always look at absolute profit
Channel mix shift is a silent margin killer in QSR
Fixed cost leverage cuts both ways — expansion amplifies losses when unit economics are weak
common_mistakes: Focusing only on food costs and missing the rent/location story
Not asking about channel-level profitability separately
Recommending revenue growth without addressing structural cost issues
